```
---
name: web-design-guidelines
description: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".
metadata:
  author: vercel
  version: "1.0.0"
  argument-hint: <file-or-pattern>
---

# Web Interface Guidelines

Review files for compliance with Web Interface Guidelines.

## How It Works

1. Fetch the latest guidelines from the source URL below
2. Read the specified files (or prompt user for files/pattern)
3. Check against all rules in the fetched guidelines
4. Output findings in the terse `file:line` format

## Guidelines Source

Fetch fresh guidelines before each review:

```
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vercel-labs/web-interface-guidelines/main/command.md
```

Use WebFetch to retrieve the latest rules. The fetched content contains all the rules and output format instructions.

## Usage

When a user provides a file or pattern argument:
1. Fetch guidelines from the source URL above
2. Read the specified files
3. Apply all rules from the fetched guidelines
4. Output findings using the format specified in the guidelines

If no files specified, ask the user which files to review.
```

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-03-01 10:47:42Z)**_

**Findings**
1. High: Global highlight key can be clobbered across component instances.  
[apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:351](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:351) and [apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:359](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:359) use a fixed registry name (`comment-highlight-active`) and delete it whenever local `popoverState` is null. If two `CommentAnchorHighlight` instances exist in the same document, one instance can remove the other’s active highlight.

2. Medium: Active highlight visual precedence is not guaranteed when overlapping base highlight.  
[apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:333](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:333) and [apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:359](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:359) register two highlights over the same range without explicit priority. Depending on re-registration timing, active styling may be partially overridden by `comment-highlight` (especially after dependency-triggered reruns).

3. Low: Active highlight lifecycle is only tied to `popoverState`, not content/range validity changes.  
[apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:343](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:343) does not react to `contentEl`, `comments`, or `currentLang` changes. If underlying DOM/ranges shift while popover stays open, the active highlight can become stale.

**Assumptions / Questions**
1. Assumed there can be more than one comment module mounted per page. If that is impossible by design, finding #1 becomes low risk.
2. Assumed the intended behavior is for active highlight to always dominate base highlight style.

**Quick quality note**
API feature-gating and cleanup patterns are otherwise solid for single-instance usage, and the new style block itself is coherent. I did not run runtime/browser tests in this review.
